claims.title: Paper-grade routing evaluation disables caching by default
claims.statement: Unless noted otherwise, the paper-grade routing workflow disables in-network caching and uses csSize=0 to isolate routing effects from cache hits.
notes: This is a workflow-semantics claim, not a publishable quantitative result.
A smoke run is acceptable here because the claim is about default mode wiring rather than paper-grade performance.

claims.title: Archived Figs. 1 and 2 are cache-enabled SANR workflow evidence
claims.statement: The archived repository bundle for Figs. 1 and 2 comes from the cache-enabled SANR workflow and should not be conflated with cache-disabled routing-only paper evidence.
notes: This claim is about figure provenance and workflow semantics.
It remains supported even though the corresponding quantitative conclusions stay provisional until rerun under the paper-grade suite.

claims.title: Centralized directory is plotted only as an oracle upper bound
claims.statement: The centralized directory curve is an oracle upper bound on intent-to-name resolution quality rather than a decentralized routing baseline.
notes: This is an interpretation guardrail for Fig. 1.

claims.title: Exact-NDN is a syntactic reference rather than an apples-to-apples intent-discovery comparator
claims.statement: When Exact-NDN is provisioned with stronger naming knowledge, it should be read as a syntactic reference point rather than a directly like-for-like latency or state comparator for intent-driven discovery.
notes: This is a constraint on how latency and correctness plots should be read.

claims.title: Bounded advertisements trade correctness against control overhead
claims.statement: Increasing the advertisement budget M generally improves domain coverage, while increasing K raises the chance that a relevant domain is probed at the cost of more discovery traffic.
notes: The current archived figure exists, but the paper itself already limits it to the cache-enabled SANR workflow.
No canonical paper-grade accuracy runs are promoted under results/runs yet.

claims.title: Selective probing improves retrieval efficiency relative to high-overhead search
claims.statement: Selective probing reduces unnecessary traversal compared to flooding, but current archived latency evidence remains cache-enabled and should not be read as a routing-only latency result.
notes: The figure file exists, but the repository does not yet contain promoted paper-grade latency runs or a regenerated routing-only latency plot.

claims.title: Hop count remains localized as offered load increases
claims.statement: Average hop count under offered load cannot currently be defended because the canonical Fig. 3 artifact is missing and no paper-grade load aggregate is promoted.
notes: Missing figure file is a hard blocker.
A paper-grade load rerun and promoted aggregate are still required.

claims.title: Routing state scales with bounded per-domain advertisements
claims.statement: The bounded-state scaling claim remains blocked because the canonical Fig. 4 file is missing and the only promoted scaling run is exploratory rather than paper-grade.
notes: The promoted legacy scaling run helps trace historical evidence, but it remains exploratory and cache-enabled.
The figure file referenced by the paper is still missing.

claims.title: Recovery under churn and failures is currently unverified in the canonical paper tree
claims.statement: The churn, link-failure, and domain-failure recovery claims remain blocked until the canonical Fig. 5 panels and paper-grade failure runs are regenerated and promoted.
notes: All three referenced figure panels are missing from paper/figs.
No canonical paper-grade failure runs are promoted under results/runs yet.
